exp_gain_formula.formula: floor(a * t * b * e * L / (7 * s))

t.name: Trade bonus
t.notes: Pokemon obtained via trade gain 50% more EXP

L.name: Level of defeated Pokemon
L.description: The level of the fainted opposing Pokemon

exp_share_mechanics.description: In Gen II, Exp. Share is a held item. When at least one party member holds Exp. Share, experience is split between battlers and holders.
exp_share_mechanics.divisor_for_battler: 2 * (number of non-fainted Pokemon that participated in battle)
notes: 50% of total EXP goes to battle participants, divided equally among them
50% of total EXP goes to Exp. Share holders, divided equally among them
A Pokemon that both participated AND holds Exp. Share receives both shares, but due to halving and rounding gets slightly LESS than without Exp. Share
If no Pokemon holds Exp. Share, the divisor s = number of participants
exp_share_mechanics.how_to_obtain: Trade the Red Scale (from Red Gyarados at Lake of Rage) to Mr. Pokemon
exp_share_mechanics.stat_exp_bug: When Exp. Share is active, enemy base stats are halved via bit shift before Stat Experience distribution. This affects even battle participants, not just Exp. Share holders.

lucky_egg.held_by: The Pokemon receiving experience must hold the Lucky Egg
lucky_egg.source: Wild Chansey (2% hold rate in Gen II)
lucky_egg.stacking: Multiplicative with trade bonus: own+egg=1.5x, traded+no_egg=1.5x, traded+egg=2.25x
lucky_egg.exp_share_interaction: Only applies to the holder; does NOT pass through to Exp Share recipients

participation_rule: A Pokemon counts as a participant if it was sent into battle against the defeated Pokemon at any point. It need not have attacked — merely appearing on the field counts.
integer_truncation: All multipliers are applied with integer truncation (floor) at each step.

growth_rates.description: Gen II uses 4 experience groups (Erratic and Fluctuating were added in Gen III)

fast.description: Reaches level 100 fastest among Gen II groups

medium_fast.description: Simplest formula — total EXP equals level cubed

medium_slow.description: Produces negative values at level 1 (game handles this as 0)
medium_slow.quirk: Paradoxically slower than Medium Fast until approximately level 67, then becomes slightly faster. The polynomial has an inflection point between levels 4-5.
known_bugs: The formula gives -54 at level 1. Since Gen II uses unsigned 24-bit integers, -54 wraps to 16,777,162 (0xFFFFCA). If a level 1 Medium Slow Pokemon gains fewer than 54 EXP, the total wraps past 0xFFFFFF and the game thinks it is level 100. This is why Eggs hatch at level 5 (not 1) and no wild Pokemon appear below level 2.

slow.description: Requires the most total EXP to reach level 100

level_cap.behavior_at_cap: Pokemon at level 100 no longer gain experience, but the game erroneously still displays the EXP gained message (fixed in Gen III)

base_experience_yields.description: Base EXP yield (b) for each species. Cross-referenced against pokecrystal disassembly. Range: Magikarp (20) to Chansey/Blissey (255).

experience_groups_by_pokemon.description: Which growth rate group each Pokemon belongs to

day_care.evolution: Pokemon will NOT evolve in the Day-Care
day_care.moves: Level-up moves are learned automatically; oldest move (slot 1) is deleted first. HM moves CAN be forgotten this way in Gen II (unlike Gen I).
day_care.exp_loss_bug: When withdrawing, the game recalculates EXP to the minimum for the current level, discarding surplus. If no levels were gained, the Pokemon can actually lose EXP compared to deposit.

level_up_mechanics.multi_level: A Pokemon can gain multiple levels from a single battle if enough EXP is earned
level_up_mechanics.move_learning: Level-up moves are checked at each level gained. If 4 moves known, player chooses which to replace or skip.
level_up_mechanics.evolution: Evolution is checked after leveling from battle EXP or Rare Candy. Day-Care leveling does NOT trigger evolution.
level_up_mechanics.stat_recalc: Stats are recalculated at each level-up using Gen II formula with base stats, DVs (IVs), and Stat Experience (EVs)

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Medium Slow level 1 underflow
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: The Medium Slow formula produces -54 at level 1. Since Gen II uses unsigned 24-bit integers, -54 wraps to 16,777,162 (0xFFFFCA). A level 1 Medium Slow Pokemon deposited in the PC will freeze the game when withdrawn. This is why Eggs hatch at level 5.

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Level 100 EXP message
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: Pokemon at level 100 can no longer gain experience, but the game erroneously still displays the experience gained message. Fixed in Gen III.

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Day-Care experience loss
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: Withdrawing from Day-Care recalculates EXP to minimum for current level via CalcExpAtLevel, discarding any surplus above the level threshold.

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Five-digit EXP display error
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: Experience gains of 10,000 or more display incorrectly because the text formatter only allocates 4 digits of display space.

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Exp Share Stat Experience halving
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: When any party member holds Exp. Share, enemy base stats are right-shifted by 1 bit before Stat Experience distribution, halving Stat EXP for ALL recipients including battle participants.

known_bugs_and_quirks.name: Levels above 100
known_bugs_and_quirks.description: Pokemon obtained above level 100 via glitches can be leveled to 255 with Rare Candies. Using a Rare Candy at level 255 wraps to level 0 (byte overflow). Natural level-up drops back to 100.
